justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Strong signals explicitly acknowledged — observability, load testing, blue/green deployment praised throughout. (2) Non-idempotent write + retry = guaranteed duplicates identified as the primary blocker, called 'mathematical certainty' and 'not an edge case.' (3) DLQ with no consumer explicitly called 'a write-only black hole' and 'worse than no DLQ' — framed as silent data loss. (4) At-least-once + non-idempotent analyzed as a systemic data quality issue under normal operation. (5) Specific recommendations: idempotency key (event_id at ingestion), upsert/INSERT IGNORE, DLQ consumer with alerting on depth. (6) Verdict is NOT READY, grounded entirely in data correctness. (7) Pippen explicitly does not let strong observability compensate — notes observability is 'blind to the primary failure mode.'

observations.text: Excellent framing: 'The service promises event delivery but actually delivers event duplication' — cuts through the conflicting signals cleanly.
Identified three independent paths to duplicates (retry, lost ACK, circuit breaker half-open recovery) — goes beyond the minimum requirement.
DLQ analysis is sharp: 'worse than no DLQ — it gives false confidence that failures are handled.'
Correctly escalated all three issues to Shaq with actionable fix descriptions.
Database safety section adds valuable insight about cleanup cost growing over time — demonstrates understanding of operational consequences beyond the immediate bug.
Observability review acknowledges strengths but precisely identifies the blind spots (no duplicate rate metric, no DLQ depth metric, no retry-vs-first tagging).
Confidence section honestly flags low-confidence areas (warehouse engine, timeout config) without using them to hedge the verdict.

agent_output_excerpt: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"Well-instrumented with request IDs and structured logging"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse","status":"broken","issue":"Non-idempotent writes mean retries (from circuit breaker recovery, exponential backoff, or lost ACKs) produce duplicate records. This is not an edge case — it is the expected behavior of at-least-once delivery under norm
